panels.title: Upload throughput (proxy: queue arrivals / min)
panels.description: There is no per-request counter yet ‚Äî this panel approximates upload arrivals by the positive derivative of upload:ready depth. Spikes with matching dips in depth = healthy encrypt pipeline. Sustained high depth without dips = worker starved.

options.content:
# Per-request latency via Jaeger

Crypto-pipeline latency ‚Äî MinIO put, AES-GCM streaming, SMTP retry ‚Äî is traced through **OpenTelemetry spans** rather than aggregated in Prometheus histograms. Open the Jaeger UI to drill into any single transfer:

- Jaeger UI: [http://localhost:16686](http://localhost:16686) (dev only; CIDR-gated in prod)
- Services: `fylix-api`, `fylix-worker`
- Filter by `X-Request-Id` header value (echoed in the span `attributes.request_id`) to correlate a failed download with its encrypt job.
- SQLAlchemy / Redis / httpx spans are auto-instrumented; MinIO object I/O needs manual spans (planned).

# Metrics gaps

This dashboard uses the two metrics we have (`fylix_worker_queue_depth`, `fylix_cleanup_last_run_timestamp`) plus OTel traces. To get richer dashboards, add these counters/histograms in `backend/app/routers/metrics.py`:

- `fylix_transfers_created_total{status}` ‚Äî Counter, incremented in `POST /api/transfers` handler.
- `fylix_download_bytes_total` ‚Äî Counter, incremented in `StreamingResponse` body generator.
- `fylix_encrypt_duration_seconds` ‚Äî Histogram, wrap `encrypt_stream` call in worker.
- `fylix_admin_login_attempts_total{outcome}` ‚Äî Counter for security alerting (brute-force detection).
